summary: Feedback-ledger signals can drive learned retention/decay, but only through a tier-gated reducer that counts strong positive feedback, protects constitutional/critical records, and shadows decisions before live TTL or edge-floor mutation.

label: feedback_events has enough memory/query/session/confidence signal, but raw total hit_count would reward weak search exposure instead of usefulness
memory-retention-sweep has the right insertion point but currently selects expired rows without tier or decay metadata needed for safe keep/delete decisions
constitutional and critical records already have no-decay semantics in scoring, but expired delete_after rows can still be deleted unless the sweep checks tier
causal-edge strength floors need endpoint-tier joins and provenance guards so noisy auto-derived edges do not get protected at 0.7
shadow-scoring provides the right promotion model; learned retention should shadow decisions before mutating live TTLs or edge strengths
the proposed TTL bonus formula is viable only after replacing raw hit_count with weighted positive hits and limiting boosts to important/critical/constitutional or pinned rows
